question: Will anyone get a perfect score at ARML 2026?
textDescription:
51st American Regions Mathematics League Competition (May 29-30, 2026)

Resolves YES if anyone gets a perfect score on the individual round, otherwise resolves NO

Assuming no questions are thrown out, a perfect score is 10

Questions that are thrown out do not count against a perfect score for this market

If the official results posted on the ARML website differ from the results announced at the awards ceremony, this market will be re-resolved based on the official results if the resolution needs to be changed

https://arml3.com/
newsShortReason: Historical data shows few perfect scores in ARML Individual Rounds.
newsKeyEvidence: In 2025, Christopher Qiu achieved a perfect score in the Individual Round. | In 2024, a New Hampshire student scored 9 out of 10 in the Individual Round. | In 2023, a Central Jersey student scored a perfect 10 in the Individual Round. | In 2021, Srinivas Arun achieved one of only six perfect scores in the Individual Round. | In 2020, a Colorado student placed 12th in the Individual Round. | In 2019, a Colorado student placed 5th in the Individual Round.
